headline.zh: OECD 工作論文把稅收優惠問題落到可比較的有效稅率與治理設計：Pillar Two 下，優惠的形式與行政結構同樣重要
headline.en: An OECD working paper turns tax incentives into a comparable EATR and governance problem: under Pillar Two, form and administrative structure both matter

analysis.zh: OECD Taxation Working Papers No. 76 以十個拉丁美洲與加勒比海經濟體的前瞻有效平均稅率比較公司所得稅優惠。相對於一般課稅，旅遊投資的有效稅率平均降低 47%、再生能源 55%、特別經濟區 85%；同類投資可能因重疊優惠而承受不同待遇。論文把問題從單一稅率拉到制度治理：優惠分散於稅法、投資法與特區法，且由多個機關管理，因而難以追蹤財政成本與實際效果。對 Pillar Two 而言，無實質要求的所得型優惠較可能遭補足稅抵銷；連結薪資、有形資產或短期暫時性差異的措施則可能不同，2026 Side-by-Side Package 的實質本位租稅優惠安全港正落在這個分界。這篇論文不處理 MAP 或仲裁，但提供了救濟確定性研究需要面對的國內實施場景：當優惠的法源、分類與主管機關意見分散時，納稅人如何預測其 GloBE 效果並取得一致處理？
analysis.en: OECD Taxation Working Papers No. 76 compares corporate-income-tax incentives in ten Latin American and Caribbean economies with forward-looking effective average tax rates. Relative to standard treatment, incentives lower EATRs by an average of 47% for tourism, 55% for renewable energy, and 85% in special economic zones; overlapping incentives can produce different treatment for comparable projects. The paper therefore turns attention from a nominal rate to governance: provisions are dispersed across tax, investment, and special-zone law and administered by multiple bodies, making fiscal cost and actual effect difficult to observe. For Pillar Two, income-based incentives without substance requirements are more likely to be neutralised by top-up tax, whereas measures linked to payroll, tangible assets, or short-term timing differences may be treated differently; the 2026 Side-by-Side Package's Substance-Based Tax Incentive Safe Harbour sits at that boundary. The paper does not address MAP or arbitration, but it supplies the domestic implementation setting for remedial-certainty research: where legal bases, classifications, and administrative views are fragmented, how can a taxpayer predict the GloBE outcome and obtain consistent treatment?

headline.zh: UN 一手文本解封：三份工作流文件全文入庫，爭端議定書仲裁定為自選最後手段、MAP 取得保障入列
headline.en: UN primary texts unlocked: all three workstream papers parsed — the dispute protocol frames arbitration as an optional last resort and puts MAP-access safeguards on the table

analysis.zh: 換用瀏覽器特徵 headers 後，UN FSDO 談判主頁與三份工作流一手文件首次抓取解析成功（CRP.26 框架公約模板、CRP.27 服務課稅選項文件、CRP.28 爭端預防與解決概念說明，均為 2026 年 1 月下旬發布）。CRP.28 的設計要點：議定書採「完整選單＋核心機制」兩階段——核心機制對所有締約方適用且不得保留或退出，但無論核心或自選，對其他文書均無自動優先效力（§12）；仲裁定位為自選、最後手段、僅限主管機關間（納稅人非當事人）、panel 三人、原則上書面審、採 final-offer 擇一且無先例價值（§41）；MAP 條款以 UN 範本第 25 條為起點，明列「取得 MAP 不得受國內程序要求不當限縮」（§37）——與 Danon 對 MAP 取得限縮的批評直接相接；另提出無稅約情形的自選善意諮商機制（§18–19）與移轉訂價資訊近用任務小組（§49–50）。零草案預定提交 2026 年 8 月第五屆會議（§3）。CRP.26 模板第 5 條要求公平分配課稅權並「重新談判與本條不一致之既有稅約」、第 6 條為 HNWI 偵防義務條款。時程一手證實：INC-5 2026-08-03–13 紐約、INC-6 2026-11-30–12-11 奈洛比，公約與兩議定書 2027 下半年提交聯大第 82 屆會議。
analysis.en: With browser-realistic headers, the UN FSDO negotiation page and all three workstream primary papers were fetched and parsed for the first time (CRP.26 Framework Convention Template, CRP.27 Services Taxation Options Paper, CRP.28 Dispute Prevention and Resolution Concept Note, all late Jan 2026). CRP.28's design: a two-step 'comprehensive menu + core mechanisms' approach — core mechanisms apply to all Parties with no opt-out or reservations, yet nothing in the protocol automatically supersedes other instruments (§12); arbitration is framed as optional, last-resort, strictly between competent authorities (taxpayers are not parties), three-member panels, written-record proceedings, final-offer selection with no precedential value (§41); the MAP provision starts from the UN Model's MAP article and expressly requires that 'access to MAP is not unduly restricted by domestic procedural requirements' (§37) — connecting directly to Danon's MAP-access critique; a narrowly scoped good-faith consultation mechanism for no-treaty situations (§18–19) and a transfer-pricing information-access task force (§49–50) are also proposed. A zero draft of the protocol is due at the fifth session in August 2026 (§3). CRP.26's template Article 5 requires fair allocation of taxing rights including 'renegotiation of existing tax agreements' inconsistent with it; Article 6 obliges measures against HNWI avoidance and evasion. Timeline confirmed from primary text: INC-5 3–13 Aug 2026 (NY), INC-6 30 Nov–11 Dec 2026 (Nairobi), final texts to the UNGA 82nd session in H2 2027.

headline.zh: UN 稅務公約談判進入草案階段：第五輪 8 月開議，爭端議定書出現反 OECD 式強制仲裁的定調
headline.en: UN tax convention talks enter the drafting phase: fifth session opens in August, with anti-mandatory-arbitration positioning on the dispute protocol

analysis.zh: INC 第四輪談判已於 2026 年 2 月結束，第五輪定於 2026 年 8 月舉行，三個工作流預計在會前提交首批條文草案——框架公約承諾條款的改寫版，以及兩項自選早期議定書（跨境服務所得課稅、稅務爭端之預防與解決）的首份實質文本（TJN 第四輪後意見書，2026-04-03）。爭端議定書（Workstream 3）的走向值得盯：TJN 主張議定書不應納入 OECD 式的 MAP 強制拘束仲裁，仲裁「至少應為自選且非強制」，另提議把 MAP 設為稅務相關投資爭端的排他管道、建立 MAP 決定摘要資料庫。這與本桌的 MAP 爭點直接相接——若 UN 路線把仲裁邊緣化，將與 OECD Action 14 的強化路線正面分岔，也會影響 Danon 所分析的稅務投資仲裁互動。第五輪是取得 UN 一手文本、補「un-tax-convention-governance 主題僅靠二手評論支撐」這個驗證缺口的時點。（2026-07-06 補：一手文件已取得並解析，見 2026-07-06-un-ws3-crp28-primary-texts 條目；TJN 的反強制仲裁立場與 CRP.28 的自選設計方向一致，且一手文件顯示這是會內廣泛共識而非單一倡議成果。）
analysis.en: The fourth INC session concluded in February 2026; the fifth is scheduled for August 2026, and all three workstreams are expected to table first draft texts beforehand — a reworked formulation of the framework-convention commitments, plus the first substantive text of the two optional early protocols (taxation of cross-border services income; prevention and resolution of tax disputes) (TJN post-fourth-session submission, 2026-04-03). The dispute protocol (Workstream 3) is the one to watch: TJN argues it should not include OECD-style mandatory binding MAP arbitration — arbitration 'should at a minimum be made both optional and non-mandatory' — and proposes making MAP the exclusive venue for tax-related investment disputes and building a database of MAP decision summaries. This connects directly to this desk's MAP controversy: if the UN track sidelines arbitration, it diverges head-on from the OECD Action 14 strengthening path and bears on the tax–investment-arbitration interactions Danon analyses. The fifth session is also the moment to obtain UN primary texts and close the verification gap in the un-tax-convention-governance topic, which currently rests on secondary commentary. (Update 2026-07-06: primary texts obtained and parsed — see entry 2026-07-06-un-ws3-crp28-primary-texts; TJN's anti-mandatory-arbitration position aligns with CRP.28's optional design, which the primary text shows to be broad committee sentiment rather than a single advocacy win.)
